stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags, including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the field of text generation using transformer architectures.
The model is associated with Meta and utilizes standard ML formats like safetensors.

stories.title: IBM Spins Off the First Pure-Play Quantum Chip Foundry
stories.author: rbanffy
bullet_points: IBM is establishing a dedicated, pure-play foundry for quantum chips.
This move signifies a major strategic shift in the quantum computing hardware ecosystem.
The establishment of a dedicated foundry suggests a push toward scaling and commercialization of quantum hardware.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance gains.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing new methods to exploit the 'personalities' of AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from earlier, simpler forms of AI hacking.
The story highlights growing security risks associated with advanced conversational AI models.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to reframe LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of current monotonic scaling laws by incorporating non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining.
Utilizes the Shannon-Hartley theorem to theoretically model LLM capacity and performance degradation.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to reframe LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of existing monotonic scaling laws by incorporating non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining and quantization degradation.
Uses the Shannon-Hartley theorem to provide a unified theoretical framework for understanding model capacity and scaling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content references the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM runners and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Nuro is positioning itself as a potential beneficiary of being a 'second mover' in the robotaxi market.
Waymo remains the established market leader with a large operational fleet across multiple US cities.
The article discusses the competitive landscape, noting that several other companies are actively trying to challenge Waymo's dominance.

stories.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The US's major investment in quantum computing faces potential legal scrutiny.
The initiative includes the launch of the first quantum foundry company.
The article questions the immediate necessity or market demand for this new quantum foundry.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Starship V3 flight was mostly successful, but remains a work in progress.
The vehicle has not yet achieved full flight to low-Earth orbit.
The report indicates SpaceX still has significant hurdles to overcome with the system.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to target an ICEYE radarsat.
The proximity and capability described are noted as unusual for standard satellite missions.
The story touches upon geopolitical tensions intersecting with advanced space technology.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new paradigm for agent skill development, moving beyond current hand-crafted or loosely controlled self-revision methods.
The core idea is to train the agent's skill as an external state, applying optimization discipline similar to weight-space optimization in deep learning.
This represents a systematic, controllable text-space optimization approach aimed at reliably improving agent performance under feedback.

stories.title: From Raw Experience to Skill Consumption: A Systematic Study of Model-Generated Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: Language agents are improving by reusing 'skills,' which are structured procedures distilled from past experience.
Domain-level and model-generated skills are highlighted as promising for enabling fast, scalable adaptation within specific domains.
Despite growing extraction methods, there is a noted lack of comprehensive understanding regarding these generated skills.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of purely textual Chain-of-Thought (CoT) in multimodal LLMs for tasks needing fine-grained visual focus.
Proposes 'ETCHR,' a novel approach that decouples reasoning from image generation by using a dedicated image editing model.
Aims to overcome existing constraints in 'think with images' paradigms, such as fixed toolkits or noisy intermediate images.

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The paper addresses the quadratic computational cost of Visual Geometry Transformers (VGTs) used in 3D reconstruction.
The core problem is the high computational load caused by global attention layers in these powerful architectures.
The proposed solution is a general strategy involving restricting the number of key/value tokens per query to improve efficiency.

stories.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
stories.author: Joydeep Chandra
bullet_points: CHRONOS addresses critical failures in temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces, specifically related to evolving data.
The system tackles issues like stale index shortcuts, inaccurate Shapley pricing under distribution shifts, and differential-privacy budget mismanagement.
It introduces a three-layer architecture utilizing neural-ODE temporal decay for unified challenge resolution with explicit public/private separation.

stories.title: PGT: Procedurally Generated Tasks for improving visual grounding in MLLMs
stories.author: Adriana Romero-Soriano
bullet_points: Proposes Procedurally Generated Tasks (PGT) to enhance fine-grained visual understanding in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
PGT works by overlaying unambiguous geometric primitives onto images to generate dense supervision.
The framework also functions as a low-cost diagnostic tool to pinpoint the causes of MLLM perception failures.

stories.title: Human Decision-Making with Persuasive and Narrative LLM Explanations
stories.author: Murat Kantarcioglu
bullet_points: LLMs can enhance human decision-making in classification tasks beyond just accurate predictions.
The research focuses on the impact of LLM-generated narrative explanations on objective human decision-making.
Prior work suggests people find AI narrative explanations understandable and trustworthy for belief change.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling to build reliable and transparent AI systems capable of counterfactual reasoning.
The proposed FM-CGM framework unifies the use of pretrained foundation models with causal constraints for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.
It aims to overcome limitations in existing methods that fail to leverage the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of large foundation models.

stories.title: Complete-muE: Optimal Hyperparameter Transfer and Scaling for MoE Models
stories.author: Yan Kang
bullet_points: Introduces Complete-muE, a new framework for optimal hyperparameter transfer in transformer models.
Specifically addresses the challenge of transferring hyperparameters between dense Feed-Forward Networks (FFN) and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) setups.
Overcomes limitations of existing tools (like μP and SDE) that fail when MoE architecture or token counts per expert change.

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The paper addresses the quadratic computational cost of Visual Geometry Transformers (VGTs) used in 3D reconstruction.
The core problem is the scalability limitation caused by global attention layers in these powerful architectures.
The proposed solution is a general strategy focused on restricting the number of key/value tokens per query.

stories.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
stories.author: Joydeep Chandra
bullet_points: CHRONOS addresses three coupled failures in temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces: stale indexing, inaccurate Shapley pricing, and differential-privacy budget mismanagement.
The proposed solution is a three-layer architecture designed to unify the treatment of these temporal and coordination challenges.
Layer one of CHRONOS utilizes neural-ODE temporal decay to manage evolving data shortcuts.

stories.title: Training-Free Looped Transformers
stories.author: Qiang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces 'training-free looped transformers' for model enhancement.
The method retrofits recurrence onto frozen, pretrained models at inference time without fine-tuning.
Highlights that naive block reapplication degrades performance, emphasizing the importance of loop architecture design.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: Introduces FM-CGM, a new modular framework for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.
Addresses the gap in current methods by unifying causal constraints with the zero-shot reasoning of foundation models.
Focuses on advancing causal generative modeling, which is crucial for building transparent and reliable AI systems capable of counterfactual reasoning.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a stronger teacher model is necessary for effective student training.
The research investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong) in LLM pretraining.
Findings suggest that proper mixing of language modeling data can allow for effective distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new framework for developing AI agent skills, moving beyond current hand-crafted or loosely controlled self-revision methods.
The core idea is to train the skill as an external state of a frozen agent, applying optimization discipline similar to weight-space optimization.
The research claims to be the first systematic, controllable text-space optimization approach for agent skills.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of purely textual Chain-of-Thought reasoning in multimodal LLMs for tasks requiring fine-grained visual focus.
It proposes a novel approach by decoupling reasoning from image generation, utilizing a dedicated image editing model.
This method aims to overcome the constraints of fixed toolkits and the noise associated with unified multimodal methods in 'think with images' paradigms.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a stronger teacher model is necessary for a better student model.
Investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong) in LLM pretraining.
Suggests that proper mixing of language modeling techniques can allow for effective distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger.

stories.title: Decomposing Queries into Tool Calls for Long-Video Keyframe Retrieval
stories.author: Derek Hoiem
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of keyframe selection for long-video Question Answering (QA) by improving query understanding.
The proposed method, ToolMerge, uses an LLM-based planner to decompose complex queries into specific tool calls.
This decomposition and merging approach aims to overcome limitations of existing methods that rely on single-query scoring or fixed schemas.

stories.title: ChartFI: Benchmarking Faithfulness and Insightfulness of Chart Descriptions from Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Siming Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need to evaluate how accurately and insightfully Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) describe charts.
Current benchmarks for chart description generation are deemed insufficient because they use simple, homogeneous charts and shallow descriptions.
Faithful and insightful chart descriptions are vital for accessibility, cross-modal retrieval, and data extraction from complex visualizations.

stories.title: An npm Package for AI Agent Orchestration Just Shipped With Its Front Door Unlocked. Here's What the CVE Actually Reveals.
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: A new npm package for AI Agent Orchestration has been released.
The package has been found to have a security vulnerability (CVE).
The story suggests that security scrutiny is increasing within the rapidly growing MCP ecosystem.

stories.title: Microsoft Foundry Just Added CI/CD for AI Agents. Here's What That Actually Changes.
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: Microsoft Foundry has integrated CI/CD capabilities specifically for AI Agents.
The focus is on bridging the gap between rapid AI agent prototyping and reliable production deployment.
This feature addresses the challenge of version control and operationalizing AI agents in real-world environments.
